steps.title: Understand the variable definitions
steps.content: $x$ represents the number of six-month periods since the end of January 1995. So $x = 0$ is the end of January 1995, $x = 1$ is one six-month period later, $x = 2$ is two six-month periods later, and so on. The variable $y$ represents the number of subscribers.

steps.title: Interpret the point (1, 500)
steps.content: The point $(1, 500)$ tells us that when $x = 1$ (the end of the first six-month period), $y = 500$ (there are 500 subscribers). One six-month period after the end of January 1995 is the end of July 1995, and the graph shows 500 subscribers at that moment.

steps.title: Match to the correct choice
steps.content: Choice A states: The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of the first six-month period was 500. This directly matches our interpretation of $(1, 500)$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
